links.title: DebugLM: Learning Traceable Training Data Provenance for LLMs

primary_link.title: DebugLM: Learning Traceable Training Data Provenance for LLMs

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are trained through multi-stage pipelines over heterogeneous data sources, yet developers lack a principled way to pinpoint the specific data responsible for an observed behavior. This lack of observability reduces debugging to reactive patching and makes failures prone to recur under distribution shift or subsequent model updates. To address this limitation, we propose DebugLM, a framework that equips LLMs with built-in data provenance, enabling them to explicitly t
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）透過多階段管線在異質資料來源上進行訓練，然而開發者缺乏一種原則性的方法來精確指出導致特定行為的資料。這種可觀察性（observability）的缺乏將除錯降級為被動修補，並使得故障在 distribution shift 或後續模型更新下容易再次發生。為了解決此限制，我們提出了 DebugLM，這是一個為 LLM 配備內建資料溯源（data provenance）的框架，使其能夠明確地 t
papers.title: DebugLM: Learning Traceable Training Data Provenance for LLMs
papers.title_zh: DebugLM：為 LLM 學習可追溯的訓練資料溯源

links.title: Learning When to Attend: Conditional Memory Access for Long-Context LLMs

primary_link.title: Learning When to Attend: Conditional Memory Access for Long-Context LLMs

papers.summary: Language models struggle to generalize beyond pretraining context lengths, limiting long-horizon reasoning and retrieval. Continued pretraining on long-context data can help but is expensive due to the quadratic scaling of Attention. We observe that most tokens do not require (Global) Attention over the entire sequence and can rely on local context. Based on this, we propose L2A (Learning To Attend), a layer that enables conditional (token-wise) long-range memory access by deciding when to invok
papers.summary_zh: 語言模型難以泛化至超出 pretraining 上下文長度，限制了長範圍推理和檢索。在長上下文資料上繼續進行 pretraining 有助益，但由於 Attention 的二次方擴展（quadratic scaling），成本高昂。我們觀察到大多數 token 不需要對整個序列進行 (Global) Attention，並且可以依賴局部上下文。基於此，我們提出了 L2A (Learning To Attend)，這是一個透過決定何時調用（invok）來實現條件性（token-wise）長距離記憶體存取的層。
papers.title: Learning When to Attend: Conditional Memory Access for Long-Context LLMs
papers.title_zh: 學習何時進行注意力：長上下文 LLM 的條件記憶體存取

links.title: The Phasor Transformer: Resolving Attention Bottlenecks on the Unit Circle

primary_link.title: The Phasor Transformer: Resolving Attention Bottlenecks on the Unit Circle

papers.summary: Transformer models have redefined sequence learning, yet dot-product self-attention introduces a quadratic token-mixing bottleneck for long-context time-series. We introduce the \textbf{Phasor Transformer} block, a phase-native alternative representing sequence states on the unit-circle manifold $S^1$. Each block combines lightweight trainable phase-shifts with parameter-free Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) token coupling, achieving global $\mathcal{O}(N\log N)$ mixing without explicit attentio
papers.summary_zh: Transformer 模型重新定義了序列學習，但點積自注意力（dot-product self-attention）為長上下文時間序列引入了二次方的 token 混合瓶頸。我們介紹了 	extbf{Phasor Transformer} 區塊，這是一種相位原生（phase-native）的替代方案，用於在單位圓流形 $S^1$ 上表示序列狀態。每個區塊結合了輕量級可訓練的 phase-shifts 與無參數的 Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) token coupling，在沒有顯式 attentio 的情況下實現了全域 $\mathcal{O}(N\log N)$ 混合。
papers.title: The Phasor Transformer: Resolving Attention Bottlenecks on the Unit Circle
papers.title_zh: 相位器 Transformer：解決單位圓上的注意力瓶頸

primary_link.title: Bootstrapping Coding Agents: The Specification Is the Program

papers.summary: A coding agent can bootstrap itself. Starting from a 926-word specification and a first implementation produced by an existing agent (Claude Code), a newly generated agent re-implements the same specification correctly from scratch. This reproduces, in the domain of AI coding agents, the classical bootstrap sequence known from compiler construction, and instantiates the meta-circular property known from Lisp. The result carries a practical implication: the specification, not the implementation,
papers.summary_zh: 一個程式編碼代理（coding agent）可以自我引導（bootstrap）。從一份 926 字的規範（specification）和由現有代理（Claude Code）產生的一個初始實作（implementation）開始，一個新生成的代理從頭正確地重新實作了相同的規範。這在 AI 程式編碼代理的領域中，重現了編譯器建構中已知的經典引導序列（bootstrap sequence），並實例化了 Lisp 中已知的元循環屬性（meta-circular property）。結果帶來了一個實際的啟示：規範（specification），而非實作（implementation），
papers.title: Bootstrapping Coding Agents: The Specification Is the Program
papers.title_zh: 引導程式編碼代理：規範即程式

links.title: IndicSafe: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multilingual LLM Safety in South Asia

primary_link.title: IndicSafe: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multilingual LLM Safety in South Asia

papers.summary: As large language models (LLMs) are deployed in multilingual settings, their safety behavior in culturally diverse, low-resource languages remains poorly understood. We present the first systematic evaluation of LLM safety across 12 Indic languages, spoken by over 1.2 billion people but underrepresented in LLM training data. Using a dataset of 6,000 culturally grounded prompts spanning caste, religion, gender, health, and politics, we assess 10 leading LLMs on translated variants of the prompt.
papers.summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLM）被部署在多語言環境中，它們在文化多元、資源匱乏語言中的安全性行為仍 poorly understood。我們提出了首次對 12 種 Indic 語言中 LLM 安全性進行的系統性評估，這些語言有超過 12 億人使用，但在 LLM 訓練資料中卻 represent 不足。我們使用包含 6,000 個文化背景提示的資料集，涵蓋 caste、religion、gender、health 和 politics，評估了 10 個領先的 LLM 在這些提示的翻譯變體上的表現。
papers.title: IndicSafe: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multilingual LLM Safety in South Asia
papers.title_zh: IndicSafe：用於評估南亞多語言 LLM 安全性的基準

links.title: How do LLMs Compute Verbal Confidence

primary_link.title: How do LLMs Compute Verbal Confidence

papers.summary: Verbal confidence -- prompting LLMs to state their confidence as a number or category -- is widely used to extract uncertainty estimates from black-box models. However, how LLMs internally generate such scores remains unknown. We address two questions: first, when confidence is computed - just-in-time when requested, or automatically during answer generation and cached for later retrieval; and second, what verbal confidence represents - token log-probabilities, or a richer evaluation of answer q
papers.summary_zh: 言語置信度 (Verbal confidence)——即提示 LLM 以數字或類別形式陳述其置信度——被廣泛用於從 black-box 模型中提取不確定性估計。然而，LLM 內部如何產生這些分數仍是未知數。我們探討兩個問題：首先，置信度何時被計算——是在請求時即時計算，還是在答案生成期間自動計算並緩存以供稍後檢索；其次，言語置信度代表什麼——是 token log-probabilities，還是對答案的更豐富評估。
papers.title: How do LLMs Compute Verbal Confidence
papers.title_zh: LLM 如何計算言語置信度

links.title: Facts as First Class Objects: Knowledge Objects for Persistent LLM Memory

primary_link.title: Facts as First Class Objects: Knowledge Objects for Persistent LLM Memory

papers.summary: Large language models increasingly serve as persistent knowledge workers, with in-context memory - facts stored in the prompt - as the default strategy. We benchmark in-context memory against Knowledge Objects (KOs), discrete hash-addressed tuples with O(1) retrieval. Within the context window, Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieves 100% exact-match accuracy from 10 to 7,000 facts (97.5% of its 200K window). However, production deployment reveals three failure modes: capacity limits (prompts overflow at 8,0
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLM) 越來越多地充當持久性知識工作者，其中 in-context memory（儲存在 prompt 中的事實）是預設策略。我們將 in-context memory 與 Knowledge Objects (KOs) 進行基準測試，後者是具有 O(1) 檢索能力的離散 hash-addressed tuples。在 context window 內，Claude Sonnet 4.5 在 10 到 7,000 個事實範圍內實現了 100% 的 exact-match 準確度（佔其 200K window 的 97.5%）。然而，生產部署揭示了三種故障模式：容量限制（prompt 在 8,000 個事實時溢出）。
papers.title: Facts as First Class Objects: Knowledge Objects for Persistent LLM Memory
papers.title_zh: 事實作為一等公民：用於持久 LLM 記憶的 Knowledge Objects

links.title: CoVerRL: Breaking the Consensus Trap in Label-Free Reasoning via Generator-Verifier Co-Evolution

primary_link.title: CoVerRL: Breaking the Consensus Trap in Label-Free Reasoning via Generator-Verifier Co-Evolution

papers.summary: Label-free reinforcement learning enables large language models to improve reasoning capabilities without ground-truth supervision, typically by treating majority-voted answers as pseudo-labels. However, we identify a critical failure mode: as training maximizes self-consistency, output diversity collapses, causing the model to confidently reinforce systematic errors that evade detection. We term this the consensus trap. To escape it, we propose CoVerRL, a framework where a single model alternat
papers.summary_zh: Label-free reinforcement learning 使大型語言模型 (LLM) 能夠在沒有 ground-truth 監督的情況下提升推理能力，通常做法是將多數投票的答案視為 pseudo-labels。然而，我們發現一個關鍵的故障模式：隨著訓練最大化 self-consistency，輸出多樣性會崩潰，導致模型自信地強化那些逃避檢測的系統性錯誤。我們稱之為「共識陷阱」(consensus trap)。為了解決此問題，我們提出了 CoVerRL，一個框架，其中單一模型交替進行。
papers.title: CoVerRL: Breaking the Consensus Trap in Label-Free Reasoning via Generator-Verifier Co-Evolution
papers.title_zh: CoVerRL：透過生成器-驗證器協同演化打破無標籤推理中的共識陷阱

links.title: FINER: MLLMs Hallucinate under Fine-grained Negative Queries

primary_link.title: FINER: MLLMs Hallucinate under Fine-grained Negative Queries

papers.summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) struggle with hallucinations, particularly with fine-grained queries, a challenge underrepresented by existing benchmarks that focus on coarse image-related questions. We introduce FIne-grained NEgative queRies (FINER), alongside two benchmarks: FINER-CompreCap and FINER-DOCCI. Using FINER, we analyze hallucinations across four settings: multi-object, multi-attribute, multi-relation, and ``what'' questions. Our benchmarks reveal that MLLMs hallucinate whe
papers.summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型 (MLLM) 難以處理幻覺 (hallucinations)，尤其是在細粒度查詢 (fine-grained queries) 方面。現有基準主要關注粗略的圖像相關問題，未能充分代表這一挑戰。我們引入了 FIne-grained NEgative queRies (FINER)，以及兩個基準：FINER-CompreCap 和 FINER-DOCCI。利用 FINER，我們分析了四種設定下的幻覺：multi-object、multi-attribute、multi-relation 和「what」問題。我們的基準測試揭示了 MLLM 在細粒度負面查詢下產生幻覺。

papers.title_zh: FINER：MLLM 在細粒度負面查詢下產生幻覺

papers.summary_zh: LLM 近來在自動 RTL 程式碼生成方面展現出強大的能力，達到了高語法和功能正確性。然而，大多數方法側重於功能正確性，卻忽略了關鍵的物理設計目標，包括 Power、Performance 和 Area (PPA)。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個 PPA 感知、工具整合的多智能體框架，用於生成高品質的 Verilog 程式碼。我們的框架將 EDA 工具明確地整合到一個由...組成的閉環工作流程中。

primary_link.title: TRiMS: Real-Time Tracking of Minimal Sufficient Length for Efficient Reasoning via RL

papers.summary_zh: 多模態自動程式修復 (MAPR) 透過要求模型共同推斷原始碼、文字問題描述和視覺 artifacts（例如 GUI 螢幕截圖），擴展了傳統程式修復。儘管最近基於 LLM 的修復系統已顯示出有前景的成果，但現有方法面臨多項限制：僵化的工作流程管線限制了調試期間的探索，視覺推理通常在全頁螢幕截圖上進行，缺乏局部定位，並且失敗的修復嘗試

links.title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs

primary_link.title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs

papers.summary: Token pruning is essential for enhancing the computational efficiency of vision-language models (VLMs), particularly for video-based tasks where temporal redundancy is prevalent. Prior approaches typically prune tokens either (1) within the vision transformer (ViT) exclusively for unimodal perception tasks such as action recognition and object segmentation, without adapting to downstream vision-language tasks; or (2) only within the LLM while leaving the ViT output intact, often requiring complex text-conditioned token selection mechanisms. In this paper, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring (STTS), a simple and lightweight module that prunes vision tokens across both the ViT and the LLM without text conditioning or token merging, and is fully compatible with end-to-end training. By learning how to score temporally via an auxiliary loss and spatially via LLM downstream gradients, aided by our efficient packing algorithm, STTS prunes 50% of vision tokens throughout the entire architecture, resulting in a 62% improvement in efficiency during both training and inference with only a 0.7% drop in average performance across 13 short and long video QA tasks. Efficiency gains increase with more sampled frames per video. Applying test-time scaling for long-video QA further yields performance gains of 0.5-1% compared to the baseline. Overall, STTS represents a novel, simple yet effective technique for unified, architecture-wide vision token pruning.
papers.title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs

links.title: Temporal Gains, Spatial Costs: Revisiting Video Fine-Tuning in Multimodal Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Temporal Gains, Spatial Costs: Revisiting Video Fine-Tuning in Multimodal Large Language Models

papers.summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are typically trained in multiple stages, with video-based supervised fine-tuning (Video-SFT) serving as a key step for improving visual understanding. Yet its effect on the fine-grained evolution of visual capabilities, particularly the balance between spatial and temporal understanding, remains poorly understood. In this paper, we systematically study how Video-SFT reshapes visual capabilities in MLLMs. Across architectures, parameter scales, and frame sampling settings, we observe a consistent pattern: Video-SFT reliably improves video performance, but often yields limited gains or even degradation on static image benchmarks. We further show that this trade-off is closely tied to temporal budget: increasing the number of sampled frames generally improves video performance, but does not reliably improve static image performance. Motivated by this finding, we study an instruction-aware Hybrid-Frame strategy that adaptively allocates frame counts and partially mitigates the image-video trade-off. Our results indicate that Video-SFT is not a free lunch for MLLMs, and preserving spatial understanding remains a central challenge in joint image-video training.
papers.summary_zh: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) 通常分多個階段進行訓練，其中基於視訊的 supervised fine-tuning (Video-SFT) 是提高視覺理解的關鍵步驟。然而，它對視覺能力精細演變的影響，特別是空間和時間理解之間的平衡，仍然知之甚少。在本文中，我們系統地研究了 Video-SFT 如何重塑 MLLMs 中的視覺能力。橫跨 architectures、parameter scales 和 frame sampling settings，我們觀察到一個一致的模式：Video-SFT 可靠地提高了視訊性能，但通常在靜態圖像基準測試上產生有限的增益甚至下降。我們進一步表明，這種 trade-off 與 temporal budget 密切相關：增加採樣幀數通常會提高視訊性能，但並不能可靠地提高靜態圖像性能。受此發現的啟發，我們研究了一種 instruction-aware Hybrid-Frame 策略，該策略自適應地分配幀數並部分緩解了圖像-視訊 trade-off。我們的結果表明，Video-SFT 對於 MLLMs 來說並非「免費午餐」，並且在 joint image-video training 中，保持空間理解仍然是一個核心挑戰。

links.title: LaDe: Unified Multi-Layered Graphic Media Generation and Decomposition

primary_link.title: LaDe: Unified Multi-Layered Graphic Media Generation and Decomposition

papers.summary: Media design layer generation enables the creation of fully editable, layered design documents such as posters, flyers, and logos using only natural language prompts. Existing methods either restrict outputs to a fixed number of layers or require each layer to contain only spatially continuous regions, causing the layer count to scale linearly with design complexity. We propose LaDe (Layered Media Design), a latent diffusion framework that generates a flexible number of semantically meaningful layers. LaDe combines three components: an LLM-based prompt expander that transforms a short user intent into structured per-layer descriptions that guide the generation, a Latent Diffusion Transformer with a 4D RoPE positional encoding mechanism that jointly generates the full media design and its constituent RGBA layers, and an RGBA VAE that decodes each layer with full alpha-channel support. By conditioning on layer samples during training, our unified framework supports three tasks: text-to-image generation, text-to-layers media design generation, and media design decomposition. We compare LaDe to Qwen-Image-Layered on text-to-layers and image-to-layers tasks on the Crello test set. LaDe outperforms Qwen-Image-Layered in text-to-layers generation by improving text-to-layer alignment, as validated by two VLM-as-a-judge evaluators (GPT-4o mini and Qwen3-VL).

links.title: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for Efficient On Device LLM Inference

primary_link.title: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for Efficient On Device LLM Inference

top5.summary: Post training quantization is essential for deploying large language models (LLMs) on resource constrained hardware, yet state of the art methods enforce uniform bit widths across layers, yielding suboptimal accuracy efficiency trade offs. We present RAMP (Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision), an off policy Soft Actor Critic framework that learns per layer bit width assignments to minimize perplexity under a global bit budget. The policy conditions on an 11 dimensional embedding of activation statistics, weight properties, and structural descriptors, enabling zero shot transfer across model families and scales. To enable stable sub 4 bit quantization, we introduce Scale Folding, a preconditioning technique that migrates activation outliers into weights via per channel scaling and normalization layer compensation. A quality prioritized reward with asymmetric penalties and budget cliffs drives rapid convergence. On Llama 2 7B, RAMP achieves 5.54 perplexity at 3.68GB (3.65 effective bits), outperforming uniform 4 bit AWQ (5.60 at 3.90 GB) and GPTQ by 6% in size and 1% to3% in quality. Critically, a policy trained only on Llama 2 7B generalizes zero shot to Llama 2 13B and Mistral 7B, often surpassing target specific training, supporting the hypothesis that quantization sensitivity is primarily architectural. The HALO pipeline exports allocations to GGUF format for kernel free inference on CPUs, GPUs, and edge devices, retaining 99.5% of FP16 commonsense reasoning performance.
top5.summary_zh: 訓練後量化對於在資源受限硬體上部署大型語言模型 (LLMs) 至關重要，然而最先進的方法卻在各層強制實施統一的位寬，導致次優的準確性效率權衡。我們提出了 RAMP（強化學習自適應混合精度），這是一個離策略 Soft Actor Critic 框架，它學習每層位寬分配，以在全局位元預算下最小化困惑度 (perplexity)。該策略基於激活統計、權重屬性與結構描述符的 11 維嵌入 (embedding)，實現了跨模型家族和規模的零樣本遷移 (zero-shot transfer)。為實現穩定的次 4 位元量化，我們引入了尺度摺疊 (Scale Folding)，這是一種預處理技術，透過每通道縮放和正規化層補償，將激活異常值遷移到權重中。一個優先考慮質量的獎勵機制，帶有非對稱懲罰和預算懸崖，驅動了快速收斂。在 Llama 2 7B 上，RAMP 在 3.68GB（3.65 有效位元）下實現了 5.54 的困惑度，性能優於統一 4 位元 AWQ（5.60 困惑度，3.90 GB）和 GPTQ，在大小上優於 6%，在質量上優於 1% 至 3%。關鍵的是，一個僅在 Llama 2 7B 上訓練的策略可以零樣本泛化 (zero-shot generalization) 到 Llama 2 13B 和 Mistral 7B，通常超越目標特定訓練，這支持了量化敏感性主要是架構性的假設。HALO 管線將分配導出為 GGUF 格式，用於在 CPUs、GPUs 和邊緣設備上進行無核心推理 (kernel free inference)，保留了 99.5% 的 FP16 常識推理性能。
top5.title: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for Efficient On Device LLM Inference
top5.title_zh: RAMP: 強化學習自適應混合精度量化，實現高效設備端 LLM 推理

links.title: Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing

primary_link.title: Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing

top5.summary: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit latent multi-token prediction (MTP) capabilities despite being trained solely for next-token generation. We propose a simple, training-free MTP approach that probes an LLM using on-the-fly mask tokens drawn from its embedding space, enabling parallel prediction of future tokens without modifying model weights or relying on auxiliary draft models. Our method constructs a speculative token tree by sampling top-K candidates from mask-token logits and applies a lightweight pruning strategy to retain high-probability continuations. During decoding, candidate predictions are verified in parallel, resulting in lossless generation while substantially reducing the number of model calls and improving token throughput. Across benchmarks, our probing-based MTP consistently outperforms existing training-free baselines, increasing acceptance length by approximately 12\% on LLaMA3 and 8--12\% on Qwen3, and achieving throughput gains of up to 15--19\%. Finally, we provide theoretical insights and empirical evidence showing that decoder layers naturally align mask-token representations with next-token states, enabling accurate multi-step prediction without retraining or auxiliary models.
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 儘管僅為下一個詞元生成而訓練，但仍展現出潛在的多詞元預測 (MTP) 能力。我們提出了一種簡單、免訓練的 MTP 方法，該方法透過使用從其嵌入空間 (embedding space) 中提取的即時掩碼詞元 (mask tokens) 來探測 LLM，從而實現未來詞元的並行預測，而無需修改模型權重或依賴輔助草稿模型。我們的方法透過從掩碼詞元 logit 中採樣 top-K 候選詞來構建推測性詞元樹，並應用輕量級剪枝策略以保留高機率延續。在解碼期間，候選預測會並行驗證，從而實現無損生成，同時顯著減少模型呼叫次數並提高詞元吞吐量。在各基準測試中，我們基於探測的 MTP 始終優於現有的免訓練基準，在 LLaMA3 上接受長度提高了約 12%，在 Qwen3 上提高了 8-12%，吞吐量增益高達 15-19%。最後，我們提供了理論見解和經驗證據，表明解碼器層自然地將掩碼詞元表示與下一個詞元狀態對齊，從而無需重新訓練或輔助模型即可實現精確的多步預測。
top5.title: Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing
top5.title_zh: 透過嵌入空間探測實現高效免訓練多詞元預測

links.title: Discovering Decoupled Functional Modules in Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Discovering Decoupled Functional Modules in Large Language Models

top5.summary: Understanding the internal functional organization of Large Language Models (LLMs) is crucial for improving their trustworthiness and performance. However, how LLMs organize different functions into modules remains highly unexplored. To bridge this gap, we formulate a functional module discovery problem and propose an Unsupervised LLM Cross-layer MOdule Discovery (ULCMOD) framework that simultaneously disentangles the large set of neurons in the entire LLM into modules while discovering the topi
top5.summary_zh: 理解大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的內部功能組織對於提高其可信度和性能至關重要。然而，LLMs 如何將不同功能組織成模塊仍然高度未被探索。為彌補這一差距，我們提出了一個功能模塊發現問題，並提出了一個無監督 LLM 跨層模塊發現 (ULCMOD) 框架，該框架同時將整個 LLM 中的大量神經元解耦為模塊，同時發現了 topi
top5.title: Discovering Decoupled Functional Modules in Large Language Models
top5.title_zh: 發現大型語言模型中解耦的功能模塊

links.title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute

primary_link.title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute

top5.summary: Extending language models to video introduces two challenges: representation, where existing methods rely on lossy approximations, and long-context, where caption- or agent-based pipelines collapse video into text and lose visual fidelity. To overcome this, we introduce VideoAtlas, a task-agnostic environment to represent video as a hierarchical grid that is simultaneously lossless, navigable, scalable, caption- and preprocessing-free. An overview of the video is available at a glance, and any region can be recursively zoomed into, with the same visual representation used uniformly for the video, intermediate investigations, and the agent's memory, eliminating lossy text conversion end-to-end. This hierarchical structure ensures access depth grows only logarithmically with video length. For long-context, Recursive Language Models (RLMs) recently offered a powerful solution for long text, but extending them to visual domain requires a structured environment to recurse into, which VideoAtlas provides. VideoAtlas as a Markov Decision Process unlocks Video-RLM: a parallel Master-Worker architecture where a Master coordinates global exploration while Workers concurrently drill into assigned regions to accumulate lossless visual evidence. We demonstrate three key findings: (1)~logarithmic compute growth with video duration, further amplified by a 30-60\% multimodal cache hit rate arising from the grid's structural reuse. (2)~environment budgeting, where bounding the maximum exploration depth provides a principled compute-accuracy hyperparameter. (3)~emergent adaptive compute allocation that scales with question granularity. When scaling from 1-hour to 10-hour benchmarks, Video-RLM remains the most duration-robust method with minimal accuracy degradation, demonstrating that structured environment navigation is a viable and scalable paradigm for video understanding.
top5.summary_zh: 將語言模型擴展到影片會帶來兩大挑戰：表示（現有方法依賴有損近似）和長上下文（基於字幕或代理的管線將影片壓縮成文字並失去視覺保真度）。為克服這些問題，我們引入了 VideoAtlas，這是一個任務無關的環境，用於將影片表示為一個分層網格 (hierarchical grid)，該網格同時具有無損、可導航、可擴展、無需字幕和預處理的特性。影片概覽一目了然，任何區域都可以遞歸縮放，並且相同的視覺表示被統一用於影片、中間調查和代理的記憶，從而消除了端到端的有損文字轉換。這種分層結構確保訪問深度僅隨影片長度呈對數增長。對於長上下文，遞歸語言模型 (RLMs) 最近為長文本提供了一個強大的解決方案，但將其擴展到視覺領域需要一個可遞歸進入的結構化環境，而 VideoAtlas 正是提供了這一點。VideoAtlas 作為一個馬可夫決策過程 (Markov Decision Process) 解鎖了 Video-RLM：一個並行主從 (Master-Worker) 架構，其中 Master 協調全局探索，而 Workers 同時深入分配區域以累積無損視覺證據。我們展示了三個關鍵發現：(1) 運算量隨影片時長呈對數增長，這進一步因網格的結構重用而產生的 30-60% 多模態快取命中率而放大。(2) 環境預算，其中限制最大探索深度提供了一個有原則的運算-準確性超參數。(3) 湧現的自適應運算分配，其隨著問題粒度而擴展。當從 1 小時擴展到 10 小時基準測試時，Video-RLM 仍然是最具時長穩健性的方法，準確性下降最小，這證明了結構化環境導航是影片理解的一種可行且可擴展的範式。
top5.title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute
top5.title_zh: VideoAtlas: 在對數運算中導航長篇影片

links.title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning

top5.summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for training LLM-based agents, yet remains limited by low sample efficiency, stemming not only from sparse outcome feedback but also from the agent's inability to leverage prior experience across episodes. While augmenting agents with historical experience offers a promising remedy, existing approaches suffer from a critical weakness: the experience distilled from history is either stored statically or fail to coevolve with the improving actor, causing a progressive misalignment between the experience and the actor's evolving capability that diminishes its utility over the course of training. Inspired by complementary learning systems in neuroscience, we present Complementary RL to achieve seamless co-evolution of an experience extractor and a policy actor within the RL optimization loop. Specifically, the actor is optimized via sparse outcome-based rewards, while the experience extractor is optimized according to whether its distilled experiences demonstrably contribute to the actor's success, thereby evolving its experience management strategy in lockstep with the actor's growing capabilities. Empirically, Complementary RL outperforms outcome-based agentic RL baselines that do not learn from experience, achieving 10% performance improvement in single-task scenarios and exhibits robust scalability in multi-task settings. These results establish Complementary RL as a paradigm for efficient experience-driven agent learning.
top5.summary_zh: 強化學習 (RL) 已成為訓練基於 LLM 的代理的強大範式，但仍受低樣本效率的限制，這不僅源於稀疏的結果回饋，也源於代理無法利用跨情節的先驗經驗。雖然用歷史經驗增強代理提供了一種有前景的補救措施，但現有方法存在一個關鍵弱點：從歷史中提煉的經驗要麼靜態存儲，要麼未能與不斷改進的 Actor 共同演化，導致經驗與 Actor 不斷演化的能力之間出現漸進式錯位，從而在訓練過程中降低其效用。受神經科學中互補學習系統的啟發，我們提出了互補 RL，以在 RL 優化迴圈中實現經驗提取器和策略 Actor 的無縫共同演化。具體而言，Actor 透過稀疏的基於結果的獎勵進行優化，而經驗提取器則根據其提煉的經驗是否明確有助於 Actor 的成功進行優化，從而與 Actor 不斷增長的能力同步演化其經驗管理策略。憑經驗來看，互補 RL 優於那些不從經驗中學習的基於結果的代理 RL 基準，在單任務場景中實現了 10% 的性能提升，並在多任務設定中展現出強大可擴展性。這些結果確立了互補 RL 作為高效經驗驅動代理學習的範式。
top5.title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
